params.text:
The person-centred values

A set of shared values underpins person-centred care. Values are the beliefs that guide what we decide is right and how we act. In your role, these eight values should shape every interaction.

Tap through the cards to remind yourself what each value means in day-to-day practice.

params.title: Tap a card to flip it
params.description: The person-centred values behind Standard 5. Read each value, then flip to see what it means in practice.

dialogs.answer: Every person is unique. Support their own identity, culture, beliefs, likes and dislikes — never assume two people want the same thing.
Help people do as much for themselves as they can and safely wish to, rather than doing everything for them. Independence builds confidence and self-esteem.
Respect personal space, bodies and information. Close doors and curtains, cover people during personal care, and keep their information confidential.
People have the right to make their own decisions and to have their human rights upheld — including the right to take positive risks — even choices you might not make yourself.
Work with the person, their family and other professionals as a team. The person is an equal partner in planning and reviewing their own care.
Value each person’s views, feelings, background and beliefs. Listen, use their preferred name, and never talk over or about someone as if they were not there.
